findings.title: F28d: direction validator uses @field_validator instead of Annotated type alias — no case-normalization unlike LogicField
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the @field_validator with an Annotated DirectionField alias mirroring LogicField: DirectionField = Annotated[Literal["long", "short"], BeforeValidator(lambda v: v.lower() if isinstance(v, str) else v)]. The current validator rejects "Long" or "SHORT" with a validation error instead of normalising. LogicField normalises case transparently. Use DirectionField on both StrategyRequest.direction and BotConfig.direction.

findings.title: BotConfig.direction in bot_manager.py has no validator — F28d left it unguarded
findings.suggested_fix: Add a direction validator to BotConfig identical to the one added to StrategyRequest in F28d, or introduce a shared DirectionField Annotated alias in models.py and apply it to both models. Without this, malformed direction strings pass into BotConfig silently, breaking journal scoping via compute_realized_pnl (which uses direction as a filter key) and position logic.

findings.title: F29: batch quote endpoint returns empty-string symbol in error response when input is all-whitespace
findings.suggested_fix: When sym is empty after strip(), the code stores {"symbol": sym} where sym is "". The single endpoint raises HTTP 400 for this case. For consistency, either skip the entry or preserve the original (pre-strip) value in the error response so callers can correlate it back to their input.

findings.title: F30: test_sequential_calls_each_fetch assertion overstates — in production the second sequential call hits TTL cache, not _fetch
findings.suggested_fix: The test correctly verifies the dedup dict is cleaned up after Future completion (so a second call creates a new Future). However the docstring says "sequential calls must each invoke _fetch" which is false in production — the TTL cache in _fetch() would serve the second call from memory. The test is correct for what it tests; add a comment clarifying it tests the dedup-layer cleanup only, not end-to-end provider call count.

findings.title: F31: eval_rules with unexpected logic value silently falls through to any() (OR semantics) — Literal annotation does not guard at runtime
findings.suggested_fix: Python does not enforce Literal at runtime for function parameters. If any caller passes an unexpected value the expression all(results) if logic=="AND" else any(results) silently uses OR semantics. All current callers are guarded by LogicField so this is low-probability, but a defensive assert or raise at the bottom of eval_rules would catch future regressions. Pre-existing issue; the F31 annotation change is correct and improves mypy/IDE checking.

residual_risks: BotConfig.direction in bot_manager.py has no case-normalization or validation. Any code path passing a non-lowercase direction string silently accepts it and produces wrong journal scoping and position logic (compute_realized_pnl uses direction as a filter key). This is the highest residual risk from F28d.

The F30 concurrent dedup test uses time.sleep(0.05) in a thread executor to ensure the Future is still pending when the second coroutine runs. On a slow or loaded CI machine this timing assumption could fail. An asyncio.Event barrier would be more deterministic.
F29 batch validation does not reject non-printable or control characters in ticker symbols — only length and emptiness. Acceptable for internal use but a gap if the endpoint is ever exposed publicly.
testing_gaps: No test covers StrategyRequest instantiation with direction="Short" or "LONG" (mixed case) to reveal the case-normalization gap.
No test covers BotConfig instantiation with an invalid direction string — the missing validator in bot_manager.py is untested.
No test for the F29 batch endpoint with all-whitespace ticker input to verify the empty-string symbol in error response.
No test for eval_rules with an unexpected logic value (e.g. "XOR") to verify it does not silently produce wrong results.
